rationale: The citing paper’s core question is the trade-off of using consumer reports in certification and it explicitly studies buyer valuation/retention effects from that information basis, while the cited paper provides seminal evidence that customer reviews (consumer-generated information) affect sales outcomes. Given this conceptual overlap, the cited work is plausibly a foundational mechanism/background source rather than an obviously incidental mention, and the sparse raw evidence (DOI-only match) is not enough to safely drop.
